chapter_context.summary: Directs a comforting prophecy of hope to the mountains of Israel, promising agricultural fertility, the removal of national shame, and the sprinkling of clean water to cleanse their hearts, replacing their heart of stone with a heart of flesh.

outline.text: Agricultural and National Blessings Restored to Mountains of Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Agricultural and National Blessings Restored to Mountains of Israel (36:1-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Past Defilement of Land and Action for His Holy Name's Sake
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Past Defilement of Land and Action for His Holy Name's Sake (36:16-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Glorious Covenant: Sprinkling Clean Water and New Heart of Flesh
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Glorious Covenant: Sprinkling Clean Water and New Heart of Flesh (36:22-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Rebuilt Cities Made like the Garden of Eden
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuilt Cities Made like the Garden of Eden (36:33-38), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies of Hope and Restoration
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies of hope and restoration, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 36, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
